title: rubus × exalbatus
intro: Rubus × exalbatus, also known as the white-flowered raspberry, is a hybrid plant species within the Rubus genus, resulting from the cross between Rubus idaeus (wild raspberry) and Rubus fruticosus (bramble). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus × exalbatus include reducing inflammation and pain, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, or consumed as a fresh fruit, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
